post.content:
Guys can you help me out with this problem..It was asked in one of the Uber's Online assessment (ON CAMPUS hiring) in 2020.
![image](https://assets.leetcode.com/users/images/49998c94-fb83-4846-80af-12859b37fe4a_1631319447.3814433.png)


![image](https://assets.leetcode.com/users/images/67d70f1f-c400-49e3-bc17-dd3788b43068_1631319465.3525538.png)

Solution in Ruby:
```ruby
def xorProblem(array, queries)
  n = array.length
  dp = Array.new(n+1) { Array.new(n+1, -1) }

  dp[0] = array
  for i in (1..n) do
    (n-i).times do |j|
      dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j] ^ dp[i-1][j+1]
    end
  end

  for i in (1..n) do
    (n-i).times do |j|
      dp[i][j] = [dp[i-1][j],dp[i-1][j+1], dp[i][j]].max
    end
  end

  res = []
  queries.each do |l, r|
    res << dp[r-l][l-1]
  end
  res
end

p xorProblem([1,2,4,8], [[1,4], [1,2], [3,4]]) # => [15, 3, 12]
p xorProblem([1,2,4,8,16,32], [[1,6], [2,5], [3,4], [1,2]]) # => [60, 30, 12, 3]
p xorProblem([8, 4, 1], [[2,3], [1,2]]) # => [5, 12]
```

For questions where you have 20 minutes without guidance to solve you can't even think about it. Just find the recurrence formula and go.
```python
def solution(arr, queries)->int:
	# This is a dp question
	# if i = 1, f = arr[1]
	# else, f = max(f(arr[1] ^ arr[1] ^, ... , arr[r - 1] ^ arr[r]))
	n = len(arr)
	dp=[[-1] * (n+1) for _ in range(n+1)]
 
	dp[0] = arr
	for i in range(1,n):
	    for j in range(n-i):
	    	dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j] ^ dp[i-1][j+1]
	 
	for i in range(1,n):
	    for j in range(n-i):
	        dp[i][j] = max(dp[i-1][j],dp[i-1][j+1], dp[i][j])
	 
	q = len(queries)
	for i in range(q):
		l, r = queries[i]
		print(dp[r - l][l - 1])

solution([1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 32], [[1, 6], [2, 5], [3, 4], [1, 2]])
```
I don\'t understand how the maximum for [1,6] with arr = [1,2,4,8,16,32] is = 60 ?? Isn\'t the max 16 ^ 32 = 48?. The description does not make sense.
This can be solved with segment tree
